description_long: This object is a single, vibrant red flower with a dense cluster of layered petals forming a rounded bloom. The petals are arranged in multiple whorls, overlapping each other to create a full, textured appearance, with the outer petals slightly larger and more spread out than the inner ones. The flower is attached to a slender, elongated stem that is light brown and slightly curved, giving it a natural, organic posture. No leaves are visible along the stem, emphasizing the solitary nature of the bloom. The surface of the petals appears matte and slightly rough, typical of many real flowers, while the stem has a smoother, woody texture. The overall impression is of a decorative or wildflower, suitable for placement in urban parks, gardens, or as part of a streetscape planting.
description: A single red flower with densely layered petals and a slender, curved stem, lacking visible leaves. The bloom is full and rounded, with a natural, matte finish.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full, rounded bloom with layered red petals and a straight, slender stem.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the depth of the petal layers and the gentle curve of the stem.
description_view_2: Angled view emphasizing the overlapping petals and the flower's slightly downward-facing orientation.
description_view_3: Rear-side view showing the back of the bloom and the full curvature of the stem.
attribute_petal_arrangement: The petals are arranged in a dense, whorled configuration with multiple overlapping layers radiating outward from the center, creating a rounded, symmetrical bloom typical of many composite or double flowers.
